key_phrases: Information Management Best Practices quick reference card
EVA Domain Assistant Accelerator Program   File Requirements
document.title: Welcome to the EVA Domain Assistant Accelerator Program
document.translated_title: Welcome to the EVA Domain Assistant Accelerator Program
document.content:
Welcome to the EVA Domain Assistant Accelerator Program 
  
  
 File Requirements:

Use a replicable naming convention for your documents.

If you don’t have a naming convention, we recommend reviewing this Information Management Best Practices quick reference card for consideration. This support tool for naming files may also be helpful.

Tagging:

In the EVA Domain Assistant, tagging is the process of adding descriptive labels to uploaded documents for easy classification, organization, and retrieval. These tags help the system pull out specific files in response to queries and understand the document's context to give relevant answers. You can tag documents at the time of upload.

Prepare for Success with EVA

We recommend reviewing the following resources to help you maximize your use of EVA:

Prompt Guide: Learn how to craft effective prompts for interacting with EVA.

Creating Effective Prompts - Dec 2024 - EN.docx

Creating Effective Prompts - Dec 2024 - FR.docx

Templates for Use-Cases and Test-Cases: Use these to define your specific needs and prepare test scenarios for the assistant. Remove or edit columns as needed for readability.

These resources will ensure you’re ready to get the most out of your experience with EVA.

document.title: https://kmt-ogc.service.gc.ca/en/knowledgebase/article-latest?pid=KA-05215&cid=CAT-02016
document.content:
https://kmt-ogc.service.gc.ca/en/knowledgebase/article-latest?pid=KA-05215&cid=CAT-02016 
  
  
 reserve the most prioritized task. Note: The Get Next Task... from a manual work queue is to be generally used when working on manual workload or campaign queues and you must be subscribed to the manual work queue by your team lead. Tasks are grouped by High, Medium and Low priority and sorted within the priority group as follows: Receipt Date (oldest to newest) Creation Date (oldest to newest) Due Date (oldest to newest) Note: When a Mailroom Clerk receives a new document for upload, Cúram will create a new task which will go to the allocation batch. If the new task's Skill Type matches the Skill Type of the officer allocated to another task for the same client, Cúram will bundle the tasks to the officer. Note: If you are usually assigned work through automatic allocation, you need to be aware that there is a Maximum Task Threshold rule applied in Curam. This means that if your Open Tasks reach the max task threshold of 30 tasks, the batch run will no longer seek to reserve tasks for you. When the automatic allocation batch has assigned a task to a user, it will scan the remaining unreserved tasks for the same client to see which can be bundled to the same user provided they have the skillset to complete the task. Bundling rules do not validate against the max task threshold (30 tasks), meaning that a user can still receive a task via bundling if they are at their max task limit (30 tasks). Top tab: Step by Step What you need to do H2 Exp-coll: Get the next task from the Inbox using Get Next Task… In Cúram, go to Inbox . Click Get Next Task… at the top right of the page. The Get Next Task window opens. Click Yes . Cúram adds the selected task to your Open Tasks list to reserve it. Note: Cúram identifies the task that can be reserved to you based on the matches to your skills type, language and region. H2 Exp-coll: Get the next task from the manual work queue In Cúram, go to Inbox . Click the Shortcuts panel on the left side of the screen. Click Work Queues and
